author: w3Lb
content: DIAGNOSED both failures. (1) Windows tests: #97's parallel workers overlap — worker A holds the LockFileEx region on data.txt.ripsed.lock while worker B, which DISCOVERED that sentinel as an editable file, tries to read it; Windows region locks are mandatory (Unix flock advisory — why Linux/macOS never saw it), and #100 correctly turned the per-file read error into exit 2, unmasking it. Fix: discovery must never yield *.ripsed.lock sentinels as targets. (2) fuzz_engine OOM (reproduced locally, artifact saved): splice path infinite loop — empty literal find yields zero-width matches AT the newline byte (span.start == line_end), the group loop consumes nothing, outer loop appends Changes forever. Also semantically wrong (buffer boundary positions don't exist per-line). Fix: splice_eligible requires non-empty find (empty patterns keep the line path = old behavior) + defensive no-progress guard with debug_assert. Proptest gap: pattern strategy was {1,8}, never empty — widening.
